items.title: Longhorns bounce back from 1st loss, take opening SEC series over Ole Miss
items.summary: The No. 2 Longhorns punctuated the series victory with an 8-2 win Sunday behind the left arm of Dylan Volantis. He struck out a career-high 11 in six innings, allowing one run on five hits with a walk. Sam Cozart earned his first collegiate save the long way, throwing three innings, allowing an unearned run with five strikeouts.

items.title: Longhorns earn top seed in Fort Worth region for women's March Madness
items.summary: The Longhorns will open the tournament in Austin, hosting the first and second rounds March 20-23 at Moody Center. With the No. 1 seed in the Fort Worth 3 region and No. 3 overall, Texas will face a play-in opponent, either Missouri State or Stephen F. Austin, on March 20. The Bears and Lumberjacks will play at Moody Center on Wednesday to decide who gets that official No. 16 seed.

items.title: Data centers emerge as growing wedge issue in midterm races
items.summary: The tech industry’s expansion of data centers is emerging as a wedge issue in the midterms, as candidates weigh economic opportunity against the associated strain on voters’ utility bills.   In the Michigan governor’s race, Secretary of State Jocelyn Benson (D), whose husband is a top executive at a company pressing for a new data center, wants regulations addressing related water usage and energy costs. Other candidates including Republican Tom Leonard are going even further, pushing for a temporary moratorium on building [...]

items.title: State of Texas: Rising cost of oil hits Texans at the gas pump
items.summary: Many Texans are starting to feel the effect of rapidly rising gasoline prices. According to AAA, the statewide average price for a gallon of gas reached $3.29 as of Friday. items.title: United States Seeks Forfeiture of Oil Tanker and 1.8M Barrels of Crude Oil That Supported Iran and Venezuela
items.summary: A complaint has been filed in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia seeking to forfeit the Motor Tanker Skipper – a crude oil tanker seized by the United States on the high seas in December 2025 – and approximately 1.8 million barrels of crude oil cargo supplied by Petróleos de Venezuela, S.A. (PdVSA), the state-owned oil company of Venezuela. As alleged, the Skipper and its cargo are forfeitable as property affording a person a source of influence over the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), including the IRGC-Qods Force (IRGC-QF), a designated Foreign Terrorist Organizations (FTO).

metadata_.title: United States Seeks Forfeiture of Oil Tanker and 1.8M Barrels of Crude Oil That Supported Iran and Venezuela

(PdVSA), the state-owned oil company of Venezuela. As alleged, the Skipper and its cargo are forfeitable as property affording a person a source of influence over the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), including the IRGC-Qods Force (IRGC-QF), a designated Foreign Terrorist Organizations (FTO).
